intent: Write a buying guide for a parent shopping for a first baby on a tight budget. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) the One Stop Market shopping site — browse the baby category and collect at least 4 distinct products (crib accessory, feeding item, clothing, and one bath or health-related item). For each, record name, price, star rating, and review count from the product detail page. (2) Reddit — browse /f/personalfinance AND /f/LifeProTips for posts where users share advice on buying baby gear, avoiding waste, or prioritizing essentials on limited funds; collect at least 3 posts with title, score, and comment count. The report must: (a) present a prioritized 'week-one essentials' list with exact product links and a running total, (b) highlight at least two items the community explicitly warns are not worth buying new, (c) identify any storefront product that has a high star rating but contradicts community wisdom, (d) conclude with a 'what we would skip' section. Do NOT recommend a specific brand by name unless you pulled it from a real storefront page. Every price, rating, or community claim needs a markdown citation to the source URL.
author_notes: Shopping (baby category) + Reddit (personalfinance + LifeProTips). Classic budget-allocation cross-site task; forces the agent to resolve conflicts between high storefront ratings and community skepticism.
